title: sonchus maritimus subsp. maritimus
intro: Sonchus maritimus subsp. maritimus, also known as sea sowthistle, is a subspecies of the Sonchus maritimus plant, which is a type of thistle that grows in coastal areas and is characterized by its bright yellow flowers and coarse, hairy leaves. Traditionally, the plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which have been used to treat a range of ailments from wounds and skin conditions to digestive issues. The health benefits of Sonchus maritimus subsp. maritimus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a popular choice for herbal remedies. Sonchus maritimus subsp. maritimus can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
